seoTitle: Reconnect a Broken Bank Feed in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Fix a disconnected or error-showing bank feed in QuickBooks Online by refreshing the connection and re-authorizing access.
intro: Bank feed connections can drop due to expired tokens or bank-side changes, quietly leaving your books out of date. This guide shows how to identify and reconnect a broken bank feed in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Open Bank Transactions
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Bank Transactions, to see your connected account cards.

steps.heading: Find the error
steps.body: Look for the specific account card displaying a connection alert or error icon.

steps.heading: Try a quick refresh
steps.body: Click Update on the account panel to attempt a simple sync refresh first.

steps.heading: Reconnect if the error persists
steps.body: Click the Edit pencil icon on the account card, select Edit account info, then choose Fix Connection or Reconnect.

steps.heading: Re-authorize and verify
steps.body: Log in with updated bank credentials, complete any required MFA, map accounts, and confirm the feed resumes fetching transactions.

faq.question: Why did my bank feed suddenly stop working?
faq.answer: Common causes include expired security tokens, a password change on your bank's side, or temporary connection drops between QuickBooks and your bank.

faq.question: Do I need to re-enter my bank login every time?
faq.answer: Only when reconnecting after a disconnect; QuickBooks otherwise maintains the secure token connection automatically.

faq.question: What if the Update button doesn't fix the error?
faq.answer: Use the Fix Connection or Reconnect option from the account's Edit menu to launch the full reconnection wizard.

faq.question: How do I confirm the feed is working again after reconnecting?
faq.answer: Check that your For Review queue starts populating with new transactions after completing the reconnection steps.
